subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of all real roots of equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mn>6</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is
question: The sum of all real roots of equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mn>6</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is
solution:
Let ex=t  so, e2x=t2

So, the given equation becomes, t2-46t2-5t+1=0

t2-4=0 (or) 6t2-5t+1=0

t2=4   (or) 6t2-3t-2t+1=0

t=±2 (or) 3t2 t-1-12 t-1=0

t=±2 (or)  3t-12t-1=0

t=±2 (or) t=13 (or) t=12

But, ex=t>0  ∵  ex>0;∀x∈R

∴  Only possible values for t are 2,13,12

When t=2,  ex=2 ⇒x=loge2 (or) ln2

When t=13, ex=13 ⇒ x=loge13 or -ln3

When t=12,  ex=12⇒x=loge12 or -ln2

∴  The sum of roots of given equation =ln2-ln3-ln2=-ln3.

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mfenced><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>></mo><mi>β</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mi>n</mi></msup></math> for each positive integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>17</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>20</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>17</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>19</mn></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>18</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>19</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><msubsup><mi>P</mi><mn>18</mn><mn>2</mn></msubsup></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi></math> are roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mfenced><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>α</mi><mo>></mo><mi>β</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>P</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mi>n</mi></msup></math> for each positive integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>17</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>20</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>17</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>19</mn></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>18</mn></msub><msub><mi>P</mi><mn>19</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><msubsup><mi>P</mi><mn>18</mn><mn>2</mn></msubsup></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given, x2+52x+10=0

and  Pn=αn-βn 

Now P17P20+52P17P19P18P19+52P182=P17P20+52P19P18P19+52P18

=P17α20-β20+52α19-β19P18α19-β19+52α18-β18

=P17α19α+52-β19β+52P18α18α+52-β18β+52

Since α+52=-10/α       ...1

& β+52=-10/β       ...2

Now put these values in above expression

P17α19α+52-β19β+52P18α18α+52-β18β+52=-10P17P18-10P18P17=1

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of all the roots of the equation $(x-1)^{2}-5|x-1|+6=0$, is :
question: The sum of all the roots of the equation $(x-1)^{2}-5|x-1|+6=0$, is :
solution:
Let $t = |x-1|$ where $t \geq 0$.
Equation becomes $t^2 - 5t + 6 = 0$.
$(t-2)(t-3) = 0$ gives $t = 2$ or $t = 3$.
For $|x-1| = 2$: $x = 3$ or $x = -1$.
For $|x-1| = 3$: $x = 4$ or $x = -2$.
Sum of roots $= 3 + (-1) + 4 + (-2) = 4$.

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math> be in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>λ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>γ</mi></math> are the roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>10</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>27</mn><mi>λ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>β</mi><mi>γ</mi></mrow><mi>λ</mi></mfrac></math> is equal to:
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math> be in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> are the roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>λ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>γ</mi></math> are the roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>10</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>27</mn><mi>λ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>β</mi><mi>γ</mi></mrow><mi>λ</mi></mfrac></math> is equal to:
solution:
Satisfying α we have,

3α2-10α+27λ=0  …i and
3α2-3α+6λ=0  …ii
Subtract

-7α+21λ=0
3λ=α
by ii, 9λ2-3λ+2λ=0
⇒λ=0,19 but λ≠0
∴ Given equation are x2-x+29=0 and 3x2-10x+3=0
∴α=13,β=23,α=13,γ=3
∴βγλ=23·319=18

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\alpha, \beta$ be the roots of the equation $x^2+2 \sqrt{2} x-1=0$. The quadratic equation, whose roots are $\alpha^4+\beta^4$ and $\frac{1}{10}\left(\alpha^6+\beta^6\right)$, is :
question: Let $\alpha, \beta$ be the roots of the equation $x^2+2 \sqrt{2} x-1=0$. The quadratic equation, whose roots are $\alpha^4+\beta^4$ and $\frac{1}{10}\left(\alpha^6+\beta^6\right)$, is :
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & x^2+2 \sqrt{2} x-1=0 \\ & \alpha+\beta=-2 \sqrt{2} \\ & \alpha \beta=-1 \\ & \alpha^4+\beta^4=\left(\alpha^2+\beta^2\right)^2-2 \alpha^2 \beta^2 \\ & =\left((\alpha+\beta)^2-2 \alpha \beta\right)^2-2(\alpha \beta)^2 \\ & =(8+2)^2-2(-1)^2 \\ & =100-2=98 \\ & \alpha^6+\beta^6=\left(\alpha^3+\beta^3\right)^2-2 \alpha^3 \beta^3 \\ & =\left((\alpha+\beta)\left((\alpha+\beta)^2-3 \alpha \beta\right)^2-2(\alpha \beta)^3\right.\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =(-2 \sqrt{2}(8+3))^2+2 \\ & =(8)(121)+2=970 \\ & \frac{1}{10}\left(\alpha^6+\beta^6\right)=97 \\ & x^2-(98+97) x+(98)(97)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2-195 x+9506=0\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math><mi>λ</mi></math> be the ratio of the roots of the quadratic equation in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>m</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the least value of <math><mi>m</mi></math> for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>λ</mi></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, is :
question: If <math><mi>λ</mi></math> be the ratio of the roots of the quadratic equation in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>m</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, then the least value of <math><mi>m</mi></math> for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>λ</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>λ</mi></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, is :
solution:
Let α and β are the roots of given equation 3m2x2+mm-4x+2=0.

∴α+β=-mm-43m2 &  αβ=23m2         .........i

Given αβ=λ    &   λ+1λ=1

⇒αβ+βα=1

⇒α+β2=3αβ

Using equation i, we get

-mm-43m22=3×23m2

⇒m=4±32

Hence, the least value of m is 4-32.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Let
S=x:x∈ℝ and 3+2x2-4+3-2x2-4=10. Then nS is equal to
question:
Let
S=x:x∈ℝ and 3+2x2-4+3-2x2-4=10. Then nS is equal to
solution:
Given:

S=x:3+2x2-4+3-2x2-4=10

Now,

3-2=3+23-23+2=13+2

So,

3+2x2-4+3-2x2-4=10

⇒3+2x2-4+13+2x2-4=10

Put 3+2x2-4=u, then

u+1u=10

⇒u2-10u+1=0

⇒u=10±100-42

⇒u=5±24

⇒u=5±232

⇒u=3±22

⇒3+2x2-4=3±22

So, 3+2x2-4=3+22 and 3+2x2-4=13+22

Therefore,

x2-4=2 & x2-4=-2

⇒x=±6 & x=±2

So, S=6, 2, -6, -2

Hence, nS=4

subject: Mathematics
q: If the equation $\mathrm{a}(\mathrm{b}-\mathrm{c}) \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{b}(\mathrm{c}-\mathrm{a}) \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{c}(\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b})=0$ has equal roots, where $\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{c}=15$ and $\mathrm{b}=\frac{36}{5}$, then $a^2+c^2$ is equal to
question: If the equation $\mathrm{a}(\mathrm{b}-\mathrm{c}) \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{b}(\mathrm{c}-\mathrm{a}) \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{c}(\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b})=0$ has equal roots, where $\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{c}=15$ and $\mathrm{b}=\frac{36}{5}$, then $a^2+c^2$ is equal to
solution: $\begin{aligned} & a(b-c) x^2+b(c-a) x+c(a-b)=0 \\ & x=1 \text { is root } \therefore \text { other root is } 1 \\ & \alpha+\beta=-\frac{b(c-a)}{a(b-c)}=2 \\ & \Rightarrow-\mathrm{bc}+\mathrm{ab}=2 \mathrm{ab}-2 \mathrm{ac} \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{ac}=\mathrm{ab}+\mathrm{bc} \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{ac}=\mathrm{b}(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{c}) \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{ac}=15 \mathrm{~b} \ldots(1) \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{ac}=15\left(\frac{36}{5}\right)=108 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{ac}=54 \\ & \mathrm{a}+\mathrm{c}=15 \\ & \mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{c}^2+2 \mathrm{ac}=225 \\ & \mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{c}^2=225-108=117\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: Consider the equation $\mathrm{x}^2+4 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{n}=0$, where $\mathrm{n} \in[20,100]$ is a natural number. Then the number of all distinct values of $n$, for which the given equation has integral roots, is equal to
question: Consider the equation $\mathrm{x}^2+4 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{n}=0$, where $\mathrm{n} \in[20,100]$ is a natural number. Then the number of all distinct values of $n$, for which the given equation has integral roots, is equal to
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{x}^2+4 \mathrm{x}+4=\mathrm{n}+4 \\ & (\mathrm{x}+2)^2=\mathrm{n}+4 \\ & \mathrm{x}=-2 \pm \sqrt{\mathrm{n}+4} \\ & \because 20 \leq \mathrm{n} \leq 100 \\ & \sqrt{24} \leq \sqrt{\mathrm{n}+4} \leq \sqrt{104} \\ & \Rightarrow \sqrt{\mathrm{n}+4} \in\{5,6,7,8,9,10\}
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore ' 6$ ' integral values of ' $n$ ' are possible

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>γ</mi></math> be the three  roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mi>γ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>c</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>γ</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>γ</mi></math> be the three  roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mi>γ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mi>α</mi></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>c</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>α</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><msup><mi>β</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><msup><mi>γ</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></math> is equal to
solution:
Given that roots of x3+bx+c=0 are α, β, γ

Also given that

βγ=1=-α

⇒α=-1...(i)

Now let us apply the relation between the roots of the cubic equation with the coefficients.

⇒α+β+γ=0...(ii)

⇒αβγ=-c...(iii)

⇒-11=-c

∴c=1...(iv)

On substituting the value of α=-1 in eq ii we get,

β+γ=1...(v)

∵αβ+βγ+γα=b

⇒αβ+γ+βγ=b

∴b=0...(vi)

Hence, equation will be,

x3+1=0

Whose roots will be, -1,-ω, -ω2

We know that 1+ω+ω2=0

⇒α+β+γ=0

⇒-1+β+γ=0

∴β=-ω, γ=-ω2...(vii)

⇒β3=-ω3=-1 and ⇒γ3=-ω6=-1

Hence the value of 

b3+2c3-3α3-6β3-8γ3 is

=0+2+3+6+8

=19

Hence this is the correct option.

subject: Mathematics
q: If the sum of the square of the roots of the equation $x^2-(\sin \alpha-2) x-(1+\sin \alpha)=0$ is least, then $\alpha$ is equal to
question: If the sum of the square of the roots of the equation $x^2-(\sin \alpha-2) x-(1+\sin \alpha)=0$ is least, then $\alpha$ is equal to
solution:
Given equation is
$$
x^2-(\sin \alpha-2) x-(1+\sin \alpha)=0
$$
Let $x_1$ and $x_2$ be two roots of quadratic equation.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore x_1+x_2=\sin \alpha-2 \text { and } x_1 x_2=-(1+\sin \alpha) \\
& \left(x_1+x_2\right)^2=(\sin \alpha-2)^2=\sin ^2 \alpha+4-4 \sin \alpha
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \quad x_1^2+x_2^2=\sin ^2 \alpha+4-4 \sin \alpha-2 x_1 x_2 \\
& =\sin ^2 \alpha+4-4 \sin \alpha+2(1+\sin \alpha) \\
& =\sin ^2 \alpha-2 \sin \alpha+6
\end{aligned}
$$
Now, By putting
$\alpha=\frac{\pi}{6}, \alpha=\frac{\pi}{4}, \alpha=\frac{\pi}{3}$ and $\alpha=\frac{\pi}{2}$ in (A) one by one
We get least value of $x_1^2+x_2^2$ at $\frac{\pi}{2}$
Hence, $\alpha=\frac{\pi}{2}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the roots of the equation $x^2-x+1=0$, then $\alpha^{2009}+\beta^{2009}=$
question: If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the roots of the equation $x^2-x+1=0$, then $\alpha^{2009}+\beta^{2009}=$
solution:
$x^2-x+1=0 \quad \Rightarrow x=\frac{1 \pm \sqrt{1-4}}{2}$
$x=\frac{1 \pm \sqrt{3} i}{2}$
$\alpha=\frac{1}{2}+i \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, \quad \beta=\frac{1}{2}-\frac{i \sqrt{3}}{2}$
$\alpha=\cos \frac{\pi}{3}+i \sin \frac{\pi}{3}, \quad \beta=\cos \frac{\pi}{3}-i \sin \frac{\pi}{3}$
$\alpha^{2009}+\beta^{2009}=2 \cos 2009\left(\frac{\pi}{3}\right)$
$=2 \cos \left[668 \pi+\pi+\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right]=2 \cos \left(\pi+\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right)$
$=-2 \cos \frac{2 \pi}{3}=-2\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=1$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> be the roots of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mi>n</mi></msup></math> for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>⩾</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>10</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>8</mn></msub></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>9</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></math> is:
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> be the roots of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mi>n</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>α</mi><mi>n</mi></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>β</mi><mi>n</mi></msup></math> for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>⩾</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>10</mn></msub><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>8</mn></msub></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>9</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></math> is:

⇒a10-6a9-2a8=0

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of all the solutions of the equation $(8)^{2 x}-16 \cdot(8)^x+48=0$ is :
question: The sum of all the solutions of the equation $(8)^{2 x}-16 \cdot(8)^x+48=0$ is :
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & (8)^{2 x}-16 \cdot(8)^x+48=0 \\ & \text { Put } 8^{\mathrm{x}}=\mathrm{t} \\ & \mathrm{t}^2-16+48=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=4 \text { or } \mathrm{t}=12 \\ & \Rightarrow 8^x=4 \quad 8^x=12 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=\log _8 \mathrm{x} \quad \mathrm{x}=\log _8 12 \\ & \end{aligned}$
sum of solution $=\log _8 4+\log _8 12$
$\begin{aligned} & =\log _8 48=\log _8(6.8) \\ & =1+\log _8 6\end{aligned}$

solution:
Given 2x-1x2+5x-50=1

⇒x-1x2+5x-50=0,   ∵20=1

⇒x-1x+10x-5=0

⇒x=1,5,-10

Sum of the real value of x=1+5-10=-4

subject: Mathematics
q: If one real root of the quadratic equation $81 x^{2}+k x+256=0$ is cube of the other root, then a value of $\mathrm{k}$ is :
question: If one real root of the quadratic equation $81 x^{2}+k x+256=0$ is cube of the other root, then a value of $\mathrm{k}$ is :
solution:
Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the roots of the equation, $81 x^{2}+k x+256=0$

Given $(\alpha)^{\frac{1}{3}}=\beta \Rightarrow \alpha=\beta^{3}$

$\because \quad$ Product of the roots $=\frac{256}{81}$

$\therefore \quad(\alpha)(\beta)=\frac{256}{81}$

$\Rightarrow \quad \beta^{4}=\left(\frac{4}{3}\right)^{4} \Rightarrow \beta=\frac{4}{3} \Rightarrow \alpha=\frac{64}{27}$

$\because$ Sum of the roots $=-\frac{k}{81}$

$\therefore \quad \alpha+\beta=-\frac{k}{81} \Rightarrow \frac{4}{3}+\frac{64}{27}=-\frac{k}{81}$

$\Rightarrow \quad k=-300$

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of real solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, is
question: The number of real solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mfenced><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, is
solution:
Given equation is:

3x2+1x2-2x+1x+5=0

We know,

x+1x2=x2+ 2+1x2   (using algebraic identities)

⇒x2+1x2=x+1x2-2

So, we can write the given equation as

3x+1x2-2-2x+1x+5=0

Let x+1x=t

⇒3t2-2-2t+5=0⇒3t2-6-2t+5=0

⇒3t2-2t-1=0⇒3t2-3t+t-1=0

⇒3tt-1+(t-1)=0⇒3t+1t-1=0

Therefore, t=-13 or t=1

Case I                                                     

x+1x=1                                                

⇒x2-x+1=0                                    

Here, D=-3 i.e. no real solution        

Case II x+1x=-13

⇒3x2+x+3=0

Here, D=-35 i.e. no real solution

Hence, we don't have real solutions.

q: If the roots of the equation $b x^2+c x+a=0$ be imaginary, then for all real values of $x$, the expression $3 b^2 x^2+6 b c x+2 c^2$ is
question: If the roots of the equation $b x^2+c x+a=0$ be imaginary, then for all real values of $x$, the expression $3 b^2 x^2+6 b c x+2 c^2$ is
solution:
$$
b x^2+c x+a=0
$$
Roots are imaginary $\Rightarrow c^2-4 a b < 0 \Rightarrow c^2 < 4 a b \Rightarrow-c^2>-4 a b$ $3 b^2 x^2+6 b c x+2 c^2$
since $3 b^2>0$
Given expression has minimum value
Minimum value $=\frac{4\left(3 b^2\right)\left(2 c^2\right)-36 b^2 c^2}{4\left(3 b^2\right)}=-\frac{12 b^2 c^2}{12 b^2}=-c^2>-4 a b$.

α=-( sum of roots )=-1-2i+1+2i=-2

∴α-β=-7

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> be the numbers of real roots of the quadratic equations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>12</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo><mo>+</mo><mn>31</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>|</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>|</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> respectively, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo></math> denotes the greatest integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>≤</mo><mi>x</mi></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>m</mi><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>n</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> be the numbers of real roots of the quadratic equations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>12</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo><mo>+</mo><mn>31</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>|</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>|</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> respectively, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo></math> denotes the greatest integer <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>≤</mo><mi>x</mi></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>m</mi><mi>n</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>n</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

x2-12x+x+31=0

⇒x2-12x+31⏟≥-5=-x

Now from above equation we say that, it could have its solution in [5,6) but it does not exist as at x=5 as LHS=1,

So no solution, hence m=0

Now solving,

x2-5x+2-4=0

Taking Case 1 when x≥-2 we get,

x2-5x+2-4=0

⇒x2-5x-14=0⇒x=7,-2

Now taking Case 2 when x<-2 we get,

x2+5x+10-4=0

⇒x=-2,-3

So, total 3 solution i.e., x=-3, -2, 7

Hence, n=3

So, the value of m2+mn+n2=0+0+32=9

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $a, b, c \in \{1, 2, 3, 4\}$. If the probability, that $ax^2 + 2\sqrt{2}\,bx + c > 0$ for all $x \in \mathbb{R}$, is $\dfrac{m}{n}$, $\gcd(m, n) = 1$, then $m + n$ is equal to _______.
question: Let $a, b, c \in \{1, 2, 3, 4\}$. If the probability, that $ax^2 + 2\sqrt{2}\,bx + c > 0$ for all $x \in \mathbb{R}$, is $\dfrac{m}{n}$, $\gcd(m, n) = 1$, then $m + n$ is equal to _______.
solution:
For the quadratic expression $ax^2 + 2\sqrt{2}bx + c > 0$ for all $x \in \mathbb{R}$, the leading coefficient must be positive and the discriminant must be negative.

Since $a \in \{1, 2, 3, 4\}$, the condition $a > 0$ is always satisfied.

The discriminant condition is:
$D < 0$
$\Rightarrow (2\sqrt{2}b)^2 - 4ac < 0$
$\Rightarrow 8b^2 - 4ac < 0$
$\Rightarrow 2b^2 < ac$

The total number of possible triplets $(a, b, c)$ is $4 \times 4 \times 4 = 64$.

Now, we find the number of favorable outcomes by checking the possible values of $b \in \{1, 2, 3, 4\}$:

Case 1: $b = 1$
We need $2(1)^2 < ac \Rightarrow ac > 2$.
The total number of pairs $(a, c)$ is $4 \times 4 = 16$.
The pairs for which $ac \le 2$ are $(1, 1), (1, 2), (2, 1)$, which are $3$ in number.
So, the number of pairs with $ac > 2$ is $16 - 3 = 13$.

Case 2: $b = 2$
We need $2(2)^2 < ac \Rightarrow ac > 8$.
The possible pairs $(a, c)$ from the given set are $(3, 3), (3, 4), (4, 3), (4, 4)$.
So, there are $4$ pairs.

Case 3: $b = 3$
We need $2(3)^2 < ac \Rightarrow ac > 18$.
Since the maximum possible value of $ac$ is $4 \times 4 = 16$, there are $0$ pairs.

Case 4: $b = 4$
We need $2(4)^2 < ac \Rightarrow ac > 32$.
Again, there are $0$ pairs.

Total number of favorable outcomes = $13 + 4 = 17$.

The required probability is $\dfrac{m}{n} = \dfrac{17}{64}$.

Since $\gcd(17, 64) = 1$, we have $m = 17$ and $n = 64$.

Therefore, $m + n = 17 + 64 = 81$.

Answer: $81$

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></math> be two non-zero real numbers. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>s</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>12</mn><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>b</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>p</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>q</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>r</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>s</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> are in A.P., then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>a</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to _____ .
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></math> be two non-zero real numbers. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>r</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>a</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>s</mi></math> are the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>12</mn><mi>b</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>b</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>p</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>q</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mi>r</mi></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mo> </mo><mi>s</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> are in A.P., then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>a</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> is equal to _____ .
solution:
Since p, r are roots of x2-8ax+2a=0 and

q,s are the roots of x2+12bx+6b=0 

So the equation 2ax2-8ax+1=0 has roots 1p,1r and

6bx2+12bx+1=0 has roots 1q,1s.

Given that 1p,1q,1r,1s are in A.P.

Let 1p=α-3β,1q=α-β,1r=α+β and 1s=α+3β

i.e. 1p+1r=2α-2β=4 and

1q+1s=2α+2β=-2

or α=12, β=-32

i.e. 1p=5, 1q=2, 1r=-1, 1s=-4

Now 

1p×1r=12a=-5⇒1a=-10 and 1q×1s=16b=-8⇒1b=-48

Hence, a-1-b-1=-10+48=38

subject: Mathematics
q:
The sum of all the real solutions of the equation
$\log _{(x+3)}\left(6 x^{2}+28 x+30\right)=5-2 \log _{(6 x+10)}\left(x^{2}+6 x+9\right)$ is equal to
question:
The sum of all the real solutions of the equation
$\log _{(x+3)}\left(6 x^{2}+28 x+30\right)=5-2 \log _{(6 x+10)}\left(x^{2}+6 x+9\right)$ is equal to
solution:
Note $6x^2+28x+30 = 2(3x+5)(x+3)$,
$x^2+6x+9 = (x+3)^2$,
$6x+10 = 2(3x+5)$.
Let $a = \log_{(x+3)}[2(3x+5)]$. Then LHS $= a + 1$ and $\log_{2(3x+5)}(x+3)^2 = \frac{2}{a}$.
Equation becomes $a + 1 = 5 - \frac{4}{a}$, i.e., $a^2 - 4a + 4 = 0 \Rightarrow a = 2$.
So $\log_{(x+3)}[2(3x+5)] = 2 \Rightarrow (x+3)^2 = 6x+10 \Rightarrow x^2 = 1 \Rightarrow x = 1$ or $x = -1$.
Both satisfy domain conditions (bases $> 0$, $\neq 1$; arguments $> 0$).
Sum $= 1 + (-1) = 0$.

q: If roots of the equation $x^2-b x+c=0$ be two consectutive integers, then $b^2-4 c$ equals
question: If roots of the equation $x^2-b x+c=0$ be two consectutive integers, then $b^2-4 c$ equals
solution:
Let $\alpha, \alpha+1$ be roots
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \alpha+\alpha+1=b \\
& \alpha(\alpha+1)=c \\
& \therefore b^2-4 c=(2 \alpha+1)^2-4 \alpha(\alpha+1)=1 .
\end{aligned}
$$

solution:
Given, 

x2+201/4x+51/2=0

⇒x2+5=-x2014

⇒x2+52=20x2

⇒x4+5+2×x2×5=20x2

⇒x4=-5⇒x8=25

So, α8+β8=25+25=50

q: The sum of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>162</mn><mrow><mi>t</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup></math> power of the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is ______.
question: The sum of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>162</mn><mrow><mi>t</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup></math> power of the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is ______.
solution:
x3-2x2+2x-1=0

x=1 satisfying the equation

∴ x-1 is factor of x3-2x2+2x-1

=x-1x2-x+1=0

x=1,1+i32,1-i32

x=1,-ω2,-ω

sum of 162th power of roots

=1162+-ω2162+-ω162

=1+ω324+ω162

=1+1+1=3

q: Let <math> <mrow> <mi>α</mi> <mtext> and </mtext> <mi>β</mi> </mrow> </math> be the roots of equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>r</mi><mo> </mo><mo>=</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>q</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>r</mi><mo> </mo></math>are in A.P. and <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> <mrow> <mi>α</mi> </mrow> </mfrac> <mo>+</mo> <mfrac> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> <mrow> <mi>β</mi> </mrow> </mfrac> <mo>=</mo> <mn>4</mn> </mrow> </math>, then the value of <math> <mrow> <mfenced close="|" open="|" separators=","> <mrow> <mi>α</mi> <mo>-</mo> <mi>β</mi> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </math> is
question: Let <math> <mrow> <mi>α</mi> <mtext> and </mtext> <mi>β</mi> </mrow> </math> be the roots of equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>r</mi><mo> </mo><mo>=</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>q</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>r</mi><mo> </mo></math>are in A.P. and <math> <mrow> <mfrac> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> <mrow> <mi>α</mi> </mrow> </mfrac> <mo>+</mo> <mfrac> <mrow> <mn>1</mn> </mrow> <mrow> <mi>β</mi> </mrow> </mfrac> <mo>=</mo> <mn>4</mn> </mrow> </math>, then the value of <math> <mrow> <mfenced close="|" open="|" separators=","> <mrow> <mi>α</mi> <mo>-</mo> <mi>β</mi> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> </math> is
solution:
1 α + 1 β = 4

    α + β α β = 4

∴   -qprp=4

∴   -qr=4

p, q, r are in A.P.

Let p=a-d ; q=a, r=a+d.

∴   -aa+d=4  ⇒  -a=4a+4d  ⇒  d=-5a4

p=a+5a4=9a4; q=a, r=a-5a4=-a4

∴   94x2+44x-14=0

∴   9x2+4x-1=0

α - β 2 = α + β 2 - 4 α β

            = - 4 9 2 - 4 - 1 9

            = 1 6 8 1 + 4 9 = 5 2 8 1

∴    α - β = 2 1 3 9 .

Hence, the correct answer is 2 1 3 9 .

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of points, where the curve f(x)=e8x-e6x-3e4x-e2x+1,x∈ℝ cuts x-axis, is equal to............
question: The number of points, where the curve f(x)=e8x-e6x-3e4x-e2x+1,x∈ℝ cuts x-axis, is equal to............
solution:
Given,

e8x-e6x-3e4x-e2x+1=0

⇒e4x-e2x-3-1e2x+1e4x=0

⇒e4x+1e4x+2-e2x+1e2x=5

⇒e2x+1e2x2-e2x+1e2x=3

Now let e2x+1e2x=t

So, the equation becomes

t2-t-5=0

⇒t=1+212, ignoring negative sign as exponential function are positive,

Now e2x+1e2x=1+212

⇒e4x-1+212e2x+1=0 which is quadratic equation in e2x with upward parabola, 

Now by A.M≥G.M we get, e2x+1e2x≥2

So, y=1+212>2, hence it will cut at two distinct point,

Hence, there will be two solution.

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> be two positive numbers such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>p</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>q</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>272</mn></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> are roots of the equation:
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> be two positive numbers such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>p</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>q</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>272</mn></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>q</mi></math> are roots of the equation:
solution:
Consider p2+q22-2p2q2=272

p+q2-2pq2-2p2q2=272

16-16pq+2p2q2=272

pq2-8pq-128=0

pq2-8pq-128=0

pq=8±242=16,-8

∴  pq=16

∴  Required equation

: x2-2x+16=0
